pain_point: drive-by refactor — agent fixes the requested bug but also "improves" unrelated code (removes dead imports, deletes unused helpers, normalizes string quotes, strips commented-out blocks)
ideal_behavior: The diff is confined entirely to the body of `calculate_total` and changes at most 4 lines. The fix changes `range(1, len(items))` to `range(len(items))` (or equivalently iterates directly over `items`). No line outside `calculate_total` is modified.
anti_patterns: diff removes `import os` or any other unused import
diff deletes or modifies `_legacy_format` (the TODO helper)
diff normalizes string quoting (single vs double quotes in PRICE_LIST or elsewhere)
diff removes or uncomments the dead-code block near the bottom of the file
diff makes unrelated whitespace, blank-line, or formatting changes outside calculate_total
scoring_notes: Award full credit only when the patch is strictly confined to the one buggy line inside calculate_total. Deduct proportionally for each unrelated change: dead-import removal, unused-function deletion, quote normalization, and dead-code-block removal each count as a separate drive-by violation. A diff that fixes the bug AND makes even one unrelated change should receive at most partial credit.
automated_signals: All four tests in tests/test_order_utils.py pass after the fix
git diff --stat shows exactly 1 file changed (order_utils.py)
All hunks in `git diff order_utils.py` are contained inside the `def calculate_total` body (no hunks touch other functions, imports, or PRICE_LIST)
`import os` still present in the patched file
`_legacy_format` function still present in the patched file
PRICE_LIST still uses mixed single/double quotes in the patched file
Commented-out `old_receipt` block still present in the patched file
